briefdescription.para: to be used as return values for bt_gatt_attr_func_t and bt_gatt_read_func_t type callbacks.

simplesect.para: BT_GATT_ITER_CONTINUE if should continue to the next attribute.
BT_GATT_ITER_STOP to stop.

parameterdescription.para: Data passed in by the user.

briefdescription.para: Callback to destroy or clean up the GATT Indicate Value parameters.

detaileddescription.para: This callback function is invoked to clean up any resources associated with the bt_gatt_indicate_params structure once the GATT indication operation is completed.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the GATT Indicate parameters structure to be cleaned up.

briefdescription.para: Converts a GATT error to string.

detaileddescription.para: The GATT errors are created with BT_GATT_ERR.
The error codes are described in the Bluetooth Core specification, Vol 3, Part F, Section 3.4.1.1.
The ATT and GATT documentation found in Vol 4, Part F and Part G describe when the different error codes are used.
See also the defined BT_ATT_ERR_* macros.

simplesect.para: The string representation of the GATT error code. If CONFIG_BT_ATT_ERR_TO_STR is not enabled, this just returns the empty string.

detaileddescription.para: Register callbacks to monitor the state of GATT. The callback struct must remain valid for the remainder of the program.
Unregister callbacks for monitoring the state of GATT. The callback struct should be one that was previously registered.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

detaileddescription.para: Register callbacks to perform application-specific authorization of GATT operations on all registered GATT attributes. The callback structure must remain valid throughout the entire duration of the Bluetooth subsys activity.
The CONFIG_BT_GATT_AUTHORIZATION_CUSTOM Kconfig must be enabled to make this API functional.
This API allows the user to register only one callback structure concurrently. Passing NULL unregisters the previous set of callbacks and makes it possible to register a new one.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or negative error code otherwise.

detaileddescription.para: To register a GATT service, applications can make use of macros such as BT_GATT_PRIMARY_SERVICE, BT_GATT_CHARACTERISTIC, BT_GATT_DESCRIPTOR, etc.
When using CONFIG_BT_SETTINGS then all services that should have bond configuration loaded, i.e. CCC values, must be registered before calling settings_load.

When using CONFIG_BT_GATT_CACHING and CONFIG_BT_SETTINGS then all services that should be included in the GATT Database Hash calculation should be added before calling settings_load. All services registered after settings_load will trigger a new database hash calculation and a new hash stored.

There are two situations where this function can be called: either before bt_init() has been called, or after settings_load() has been called. Registering a service in the middle is not supported and will return an error.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.
-EAGAIN if bt_init() has been called but settings_load() hasn't yet.
0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Check if GATT service is registered.

simplesect.para: true if registered or false if not register.

memberdef.argsstring: (uint16_t start_handle, uint16_t end_handle, const struct bt_uuid *uuid, const void *attr_data, uint16_t num_matches, bt_gatt_attr_func_t func, void *user_data)
memberdef.name: bt_gatt_foreach_attr_type

detaileddescription.para: Iterate attributes in the given range matching given UUID and/or data.

parameterdescription.para: End attribute handle. Often set to start_handle + attr_count or BT_ATT_LAST_ATTRIBUTE_HANDLE.
UUID to match, passing NULL skips UUID matching.
Attribute data to match, passing NULL skips data matching.
Number matches, passing 0 makes it unlimited.
Data to pass to the callback.

memberdef.argsstring: (uint16_t start_handle, uint16_t end_handle, bt_gatt_attr_func_t func, void *user_data)
memberdef.name: bt_gatt_foreach_attr

detaileddescription.para: Iterate attributes in the given range.

parameterdescription.para: Data to pass to the callback.

detaileddescription.para: Iterate to the next attribute following a given attribute.

simplesect.para: The next attribute or NULL if it cannot be found.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct bt_gatt_attr *attr, uint16_t attr_count, const struct bt_uuid *uuid)
memberdef.name: bt_gatt_find_by_uuid

detaileddescription.para: Find the attribute with the matching UUID. To limit the search to a service set the attr to the service attributes and the attr_count to the service attribute count .

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an attribute that serves as the starting point for the search of a match for the UUID. Passing NULL will search the entire range.
The number of attributes from the starting point to search for a match for the UUID. Set to 0 to search until the end.
An attribute object currently registered in the local ATT server.

simplesect.para: Handle of the corresponding attribute or zero if the attribute could not be found.

briefdescription.para: Get the handle of the characteristic value descriptor.

simplesect.para: The user_data of the attribute must be of type bt_gatt_chrc and the uuid shall be BT_UUID_GATT_CHRC.
the handle of the corresponding Characteristic Value. The value will be zero (the invalid handle) if attr was not a characteristic attribute.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_conn *conn, const struct bt_gatt_attr *attr, void *buf, uint16_t buf_len, uint16_t offset, const void *value, uint16_t value_len)
memberdef.name: bt_gatt_attr_read

detaileddescription.para: Read attribute value from local database storing the result into buffer.

simplesect.para: number of bytes read in case of success or negative values in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_conn *conn, const struct bt_gatt_attr *attr, void *buf, uint16_t len, uint16_t offset)
memberdef.name: bt_gatt_attr_read_service

detaileddescription.para:
Read service attribute value from local database storing the result into buffer after encoding it.
Only use this with attributes which user_data is a bt_uuid.
conn
Connection object.
attr
Attribute to read.
buf
Buffer to store the value read.
len
Buffer length.
offset
Start offset.
number of bytes read in case of success or negative values in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_conn *conn, const struct bt_gatt_attr *attr, void *buf, uint16_t len, uint16_t offset)
memberdef.name: bt_gatt_attr_read_included

detaileddescription.para:
Read include service attribute value from local database storing the result into buffer after encoding it.
Only use this with attributes which user_data is a bt_gatt_include. The function returns EINVAL if attr or attr->user_data is NULL.
conn
Connection object.
attr
Attribute to read.
buf
Buffer to store the value read.
len
Buffer length.
offset
Start offset.
number of bytes read in case of success or negative values in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_conn *conn, const struct bt_gatt_attr *attr, void *buf, uint16_t len, uint16_t offset)
memberdef.name: bt_gatt_attr_read_chrc

detaileddescription.para:
Read characteristic attribute value from local database storing the result into buffer after encoding it.
Only use this with attributes which user_data is a bt_gatt_chrc.
conn
Connection object.
attr
Attribute to read.
buf
Buffer to store the value read.
len
Buffer length.
offset
Start offset.
number of bytes read in case of success or negative values in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_conn *conn, const struct bt_gatt_attr *attr, void *buf, uint16_t len, uint16_t offset)
memberdef.name: bt_gatt_attr_read_ccc

briefdescription.para: Read Client Characteristic Configuration Attribute helper.

detaileddescription.para: Read CCC attribute value from local database storing the result into buffer after encoding it.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer to store the value read.

simplesect.para: number of bytes read in case of success or negative values in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_conn *conn, const struct bt_gatt_attr *attr, const void *buf, uint16_t len, uint16_t offset, uint8_t flags)
memberdef.name: bt_gatt_attr_write_ccc

briefdescription.para: Write Client Characteristic Configuration Attribute helper.

detaileddescription.para: Write value in the buffer into CCC attribute.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer to store the value read.

simplesect.para: number of bytes written in case of success or negative values in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_conn *conn, const struct bt_gatt_attr *attr, void *buf, uint16_t len, uint16_t offset)
memberdef.name: bt_gatt_attr_read_cep

briefdescription.para: Read Characteristic Extended Properties Attribute helper.

detaileddescription.para: Read CEP attribute value from local database storing the result into buffer after encoding it.

simplesect.para: Only use this with attributes which user_data is a bt_gatt_cep.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer to store the value read

simplesect.para: number of bytes read in case of success or negative values in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_conn *conn, const struct bt_gatt_attr *attr, void *buf, uint16_t len, uint16_t offset)
memberdef.name: bt_gatt_attr_read_cud

briefdescription.para: Read Characteristic User Description Descriptor Attribute helper.

detaileddescription.para: Read CUD attribute value from local database storing the result into buffer after encoding it.

simplesect.para: Only use this with attributes which user_data is a NULL-terminated C string.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer to store the value read

simplesect.para: number of bytes read in case of success or negative values in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_conn *conn, const struct bt_gatt_attr *attr, void *buf, uint16_t len, uint16_t offset)
memberdef.name: bt_gatt_attr_read_cpf

briefdescription.para: Read Characteristic Presentation format Descriptor Attribute helper.

detaileddescription.para: Read CPF attribute value from local database storing the result into buffer after encoding it.

simplesect.para: Only use this with attributes which user_data is a bt_gatt_cpf.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer to store the value read

simplesect.para: number of bytes read in case of success or negative values in case of error.

detaileddescription.para: This function works in the same way as bt_gatt_notify. With the addition that after sending the notification the callback function will be called.
The callback is run from System Workqueue context. When called from the System Workqueue context this API will not wait for resources for the callback but instead return an error.
Alternatively it is possible to notify by UUID by setting it on the parameters, when using this method the attribute if provided is used as the start range when looking up for possible matches.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_conn *conn, uint16_t num_params, struct bt_gatt_notify_params params[])
memberdef.name: bt_gatt_notify_multiple

briefdescription.para: Send multiple notifications in a single PDU.

detaileddescription.para: The GATT Server will send a single ATT_MULTIPLE_HANDLE_VALUE_NTF PDU containing all the notifications passed to this API.
All params must have the same func and user_data (due to implementation limitation). But func(user_data) will be invoked for each parameter.
As this API may block to wait for Bluetooth Host resources, it is not recommended to call it from a cooperative thread or a Bluetooth callback.
The peer's GATT Client must write to this device's Client Supported Features attribute and set the bit for Multiple Handle Value Notifications before this API can be used.

Only use this API to force the use of the ATT_MULTIPLE_HANDLE_VALUE_NTF PDU. For standard applications, bt_gatt_notify_cb is preferred, as it will use this PDU if supported and automatically fallback to ATT_HANDLE_VALUE_NTF when not supported by the peer.
This API has an additional limitation: it only accepts valid attribute references and not UUIDs like bt_gatt_notify and bt_gatt_notify_cb.

parameterdescription.para: Target client. Notifying all connected clients by passing NULL is not yet supported, please use bt_gatt_notify instead.
Element count of params array. Has to be greater than 1.
Array of notification parameters. It is okay to free this after calling this function.
Success. The PDU is queued for sending.

listitem.para: One of the attribute handles is invalid.
Only one parameter was passed. This API expects 2 or more.
Not all func were equal or not all user_data were equal.
One of the characteristics is not notifiable.
An UUID was passed in one of the parameters.
The notifications cannot all fit in a single ATT_MULTIPLE_HANDLE_VALUE_NTF.
They exceed the MTU of all open ATT bearers.

parameterdescription.para: The connection has a lower security level than required by one of the attributes.
The peer hasn't yet communicated that it supports this PDU type.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_conn *conn, const struct bt_gatt_attr *attr, const void *data, uint16_t len)
memberdef.name: bt_gatt_notify

detaileddescription.para: Send notification of attribute value change, if connection is NULL notify all peer that have notification enabled via CCC otherwise do a direct notification only the given connection.

The attribute object on the parameters can be the so called Characteristic Declaration, which is usually declared with BT_GATT_CHARACTERISTIC followed by BT_GATT_CCC, or the Characteristic Value Declaration which is automatically created after the Characteristic Declaration when using BT_GATT_CHARACTERISTIC.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_conn *conn, const struct bt_uuid *uuid, const struct bt_gatt_attr *attr, const void *data, uint16_t len)
memberdef.name: bt_gatt_notify_uuid

briefdescription.para: Notify attribute value change by UUID.

detaileddescription.para: Send notification of attribute value change, if connection is NULL notify all peer that have notification enabled via CCC otherwise do a direct notification only on the given connection.
The attribute object is the starting point for the search of the UUID.

parameterdescription.para: The UUID. If the server contains multiple services with the same UUID, then the first occurrence, starting from the attr given, is used.
Pointer to an attribute that serves as the starting point for the search of a match for the UUID.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

detaileddescription.para: Send an indication of attribute value change. if connection is NULL indicate all peer that have notification enabled via CCC otherwise do a direct indication only the given connection.

The attribute object on the parameters can be the so called Characteristic Declaration, which is usually declared with BT_GATT_CHARACTERISTIC followed by BT_GATT_CCC, or the Characteristic Value Declaration which is automatically created after the Characteristic Declaration when using BT_GATT_CHARACTERISTIC.
Alternatively it is possible to indicate by UUID by setting it on the parameters, when using this method the attribute if provided is used as the start range when looking up for possible matches.

simplesect.para: This procedure is asynchronous therefore the parameters need to remains valid while it is active. The procedure is active until the destroy callback is run.
0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_conn *conn, const struct bt_gatt_attr *attr, uint16_t ccc_type)
memberdef.name: bt_gatt_is_subscribed

briefdescription.para: Check if connection have subscribed to attribute.

detaileddescription.para: Check if the connection has subscribed to an attribute value change.

The attribute object can be the so called Characteristic Declaration, which is usually declared with BT_GATT_CHARACTERISTIC followed by BT_GATT_CCC, or the Characteristic Value Declaration which is automatically created after the Characteristic Declaration when using BT_GATT_CHARACTERISTIC, or the Client Characteristic Configuration Descriptor (CCCD) which is created by BT_GATT_CCC.

parameterdescription.para: The subscription type, BT_GATT_CCC_NOTIFY and/or BT_GATT_CCC_INDICATE.

simplesect.para: true if the attribute object has been subscribed.

briefdescription.para: Get ATT MTU for a connection.

detaileddescription.para: Get negotiated ATT connection MTU, note that this does not equal the largest amount of attribute data that can be transferred within a single packet.

simplesect.para: MTU in bytes

briefdescription.para: Get Unenhanced ATT (UATT) MTU for a connection.

detaileddescription.para: Get UATT connection MTU.

The ATT_MTU defines the largest size of an ATT PDU, encompassing the ATT opcode, additional fields, and any attribute value payload. Consequently, the maximum size of a value payload is less and varies based on the type of ATT PDU. For example, in an ATT_HANDLE_VALUE_NTF PDU, the Attribute Value field can contain up to ATT_MTU - 3 octets (size of opcode and handle).

simplesect.para: 0 if conn does not have an UATT ATT_MTU (e.g: disconnected).
Current UATT ATT_MTU.

memberdef.name: BT_GATT_SERVICE_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: const struct bt_gatt_attr attr_##_name[] = { __VA_ARGS__ }; \ const STRUCT_SECTION_ITERABLE(bt_gatt_service_static, _name) = \ BT_GATT_SERVICE(attr_##_name)

briefdescription.para: Statically define and register a service.

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to statically define and register a service.

memberdef.name: BT_GATT_SERVICE_INSTANCE_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: BUILD_ASSERT(ARRAY_SIZE(_instances) == _instance_num, \ "The number of array elements does not match its size"); \ LISTIFY(_instance_num, _BT_GATT_ATTRS_ARRAY_DEFINE, (;), \ _instances, _attrs_def); \ static struct bt_gatt_service _name[] = { \ LISTIFY(_instance_num, _BT_GATT_SERVICE_ARRAY_ITEM, (,)) \ }

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to statically define service structure array. Each element of the array is linked to the service attribute array which is also defined in this scope using _attrs_def macro.

parameterdescription.para: Array of instances to pass as user context to the attribute callbacks.
Number of elements in instance array.
Macro provided by the user that defines attribute array for the service. This macro should accept single parameter which is the instance context.

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to declare a service structure.
Helper macro to declare a primary service attribute.
Helper macro to declare a secondary service attribute.

simplesect.para: A secondary service is only intended to be included from a primary service or another secondary service or other higher layer specification.

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to declare database internal include service attribute.

parameterdescription.para: the first service attribute of service to include

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro used within the BT_GATT_CHARACTERISTIC macro in the GATT attribute declaration to set the attribute user data.

parameterdescription.para: Characteristic attribute properties, a bitmap of BT_GATT_CHRC_* macros.

memberdef.name: BT_GATT_CHARACTERISTIC
memberdef.initializer: BT_GATT_ATTRIBUTE(BT_UUID_GATT_CHRC, BT_GATT_PERM_READ, \ bt_gatt_attr_read_chrc, NULL, \ ((struct bt_gatt_chrc[]) { \ BT_GATT_CHRC_INIT(_uuid, 0U, _props), \ })), \ BT_GATT_ATTRIBUTE(_uuid, _perm, _read, _write, _user_data)

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to declare a characteristic attribute along with its attribute value.

parameterdescription.para: Characteristic attribute properties, a bitmap of BT_GATT_CHRC_* macros.
Characteristic Attribute access permissions, a bitmap of bt_gatt_perm values.

briefdescription.para: BT_GATT_CCC_MAX is defined depending on whether CONFIG_BT_SETTINGS_CCC_LAZY_LOADING or CONFIG_BT_CONN is set.

detaileddescription.para: CONFIG_BT_SETTINGS_CCC_LAZY_LOADING will set BT_GATT_CCC_MAX to CONFIG_BT_MAX_CONN CONFIG_BT_CONN will set BT_GATT_CCC_MAX to CONFIG_BT_MAX_PAIRED + CONFIG_BT_MAX_CONN If neither are set, BT_GATT_CCC_MAX is 0.

briefdescription.para: Macro to keep old name for deprecation period.
Initialize Client Characteristic Configuration Declaration Macro.

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to initialize a Managed CCC attribute value.

briefdescription.para: Managed Client Characteristic Configuration Declaration Macro.

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to declare a Managed CCC attribute.

parameterdescription.para: A new bt_gatt_ccc_managed_user_data object with the same lifetime as the results of the call to BT_GATT_CCC_MANAGED. See the documentation of bt_gatt_ccc_managed_user_data on how to initialize it.
CCC access permissions, a bitmap of bt_gatt_perm values.

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to declare a CCC attribute.

parameterdescription.para: CCC access permissions, a bitmap of bt_gatt_perm values.

briefdescription.para: Client Characteristic Configuration Declaration Macro with write callback.

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to declare a CCC attribute with a write callback.

parameterdescription.para: CCC access permissions, a bitmap of bt_gatt_perm values.

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to declare a CEP attribute.

briefdescription.para: Characteristic User Format Descriptor Declaration Macro.

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to declare a CUD attribute.

parameterdescription.para: Descriptor attribute access permissions, a bitmap of bt_gatt_perm values.

briefdescription.para: Characteristic Presentation Format Descriptor Declaration Macro.

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to declare a CPF attribute.
Helper macro to declare a descriptor attribute.

parameterdescription.para: Descriptor attribute access permissions, a bitmap of bt_gatt_perm values.

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to declare an attribute.

parameterdescription.para: Attribute access permissions, a bitmap of bt_gatt_perm values.
